problem_addressed: Language model agents struggle with complex software engineering tasks when using standard interfaces, unlike human engineers who benefit from sophisticated tools like IDEs.
core_claim: A custom agent-computer interface (ACI) specifically designed for language model agents significantly enhances their ability to autonomously solve software engineering tasks.
key_contributions: Introduction of SWE-agent system with custom ACI for LM agents
State-of-the-art performance on SWE-bench (12.5% pass@1) and HumanEvalFix (87.7%) benchmarks
novelty: Custom agent-computer interface (ACI) with LM-friendly commands and environment feedback, unlike previous approaches using standard interfaces like Linux shell or Python interpreter.
method_summary: SWE-agent uses a language model agent interacting with a computer through a custom ACI that includes LM-friendly commands for navigation, file operations, and execution, with structured feedback from the environment.
method_components: Search and edit functions with line-level precision
LM-friendly command structure and environment feedback
key_innovation: The agent-computer interface (ACI) design that bridges the gap between LM capabilities and software engineering requirements through carefully crafted commands and feedback mechanisms.
assumptions: Language model agents can effectively utilize structured interfaces
Interface design significantly impacts agent performance
LM agents benefit from specialized tools similar to human engineers
theoretical_grounding: References HCI studies on interface efficacy for humans, suggesting similar principles apply to LM agents in software engineering contexts.
datasets_used: SWE-bench test set Lite split (35 trajectories)
headline_result: Table 7 shows % Resolved performance for task instances from different years
best_result: Table 7 shows % Resolved performance for task instances from different years
title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
